pi_items.verified_value: INI files use key=value format for numerical values and TextVariable="contents" format for text values
pi_items.source: Bridge Command official documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.1/IniFileFormat.php) and verified against actual scenario files (environment.ini, ownship.ini, othership.ini)

pi_items.verified_value: othership.ini uses indexed notation Type(1), Type(2), etc. for vessel specifications, and nested indexing like Bearing(1,1), Bearing(1,2) for leg-specific parameters
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.1/Scenario.php) and actual scenario file benchmarks/cua_world/environments/bridgecommand_env/data/portsmouth_approach/othership.ini shows Type(1)="Tanker", Type(2)="Yacht"

pi_items.metadata_value: World Model stored in 'Setting' key in environment.ini
pi_items.verified_value: environment.ini contains Setting="Portsmouth" which specifies the world model
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation and verified in actual scenario file showing Setting="Portsmouth"

pi_items.metadata_value: Start time stored in 'StartTime' key in environment.ini
pi_items.verified_value: environment.ini contains StartTime=14.0 (decimal hours in 24-hour format)
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation and verified in actual scenario file showing StartTime=14.0

pi_items.metadata_value: Own ship name stored in 'ShipName' key in ownship.ini
pi_items.verified_value: ownship.ini contains ShipName="MV Sentinel" which identifies the player-controlled vessel
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/4.1/Scenario.php) and verified in actual scenario file

pi_items.metadata_value: Count indices in Type(N) to determine traffic vessel count
pi_items.verified_value: othership.ini contains Number=2 field for direct count, and also has Type(1) and Type(2) indexed entries. Counting indices (Type(1) through Type(2)) yields 2 vessels, matching the Number field
pi_items.source: Bridge Command documentation and verified in actual scenario file showing both direct count (Number=2) and indexed notation (Type(1) and Type(2))

pi_items.verified_value: Scenarios are deployed to /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/ (from GitHub bridge command source installation) or created synthetically by setup_task.sh if directory is empty
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and install_bridgecommand.sh scripts show this directory structure

pi_items.verified_value: portsmouth_approach scenario uses real geographical coordinates for Portsmouth Harbour, England: Latitude range 50.7750-50.7820, Longitude range -1.0980 to -1.1065. Actual Portsmouth Harbour: 50.8208, -1.1203. Scenario coordinates are within the harbourarea.
pi_items.source: Verified against actual scenario files and Portsmouth, England GPS coordinates from https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/gb/united-kingdom/27191/portsmouth-harbour

privileged_info_summary: The task involves cataloging Bridge Command maritime simulator scenarios. Bridge Command is an open-source ship bridge simulator (https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc) that uses INI-format configuration files for scenarios. Scenario configuration uses the key=value format with textual values in quotes and numerical values without quotes. The environment.ini file contains Setting (world model name) and StartTime (in decimal 24-hour format) keys. The ownship.ini file contains ShipName for the player-controlled vessel. The othership.ini file contains a Number field for traffic vessel count and uses indexed notation Type(N) for vessel specifications and nested indexing like Bearing(N,M) for leg parameters. The portsmouth_approach scenario provided in the environment data uses real geographical coordinates from Portsmouth Harbour, England (50.78°N, 1.10°W). All procedural claims in the task description about INI file parsing and data field locations have been verified against official Bridge Command documentation and actual scenario files.
